body: I see this dark theme / dark mode enhancement was closed and/or merged.  Has this been implemented in any way, even partially?  Or is it just scheduled to be?  If it's been implemented, are there any instructions or basic documentation for how to use it?  Thanks and sorry if I'm missing something.
author: kernelcrunch

body:
> I see this dark theme / dark mode enhancement was closed and/or merged. Has this been implemented in any way, even partially? Or is it just scheduled to be? If it's been implemented, are there any instructions or basic documentation for how to use it? Thanks and sorry if I'm missing something.

Hello! This was implemented and pushed to the master branch on May 21, but the latest stable release package was published on May 2, so it did not include this feature. However, if you go to https://github.com/rustdesk/rustdesk/releases and download the "nightly" version, it definitely has a dark mode! (Though it is a pre-release version, so be cautious.)

author: rustdesk

body:
> Do I have need to remove the version string from `rustdesk-1.1.10-x64.exe`?

No, you don't have to change anything in your release process.

> I still do not know how to do
> 
> > Please add a GitHub token with public_repo scope as a repository secret and rename the secret name in the workflow.
> 
> It is a pre-assigened token from you or any token I generate?

You've to goto https://github.com/settings/tokens/new and enter a token name (it can be anything, you want) and select `public_repo` in the permissions section. Refer to the screenshot below:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/83997633/173839262-42a1c827-8af2-4cb0-88fc-279a682c40c3.png)
After this, click generate token and copy it to the clipboard.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/83997633/173841062-d38bbd72-4894-4922-9bf5-8db5c1f852b6.png)

body:
> correction 2.
> 
> When you click the mouse button in two places on the screen while pressing the Command key on the Mac, the mouse input will be incorrect. I modified it.

Hey, I found an easier way to fix the mouse problem.

When the local Windows hits the win key, the remote macOS gets the command key. When you long press the win key, the command key will be continuously pressed and released. We only need to lock the state of the win key pressed to get the correct result.

It might be possible to use RustDesk as controlled side via flatpak.  
At least this is possible for the AnyDesk flatpak (just tested it).  
https://flathub.org/apps/details/com.anydesk.Anydesk

So there might be a clue how to achieve this for RustDesk too.  
https://github.com/flathub/com.anydesk.Anydesk

Maybe this!?
https://github.com/flathub/com.anydesk.Anydesk/blob/16172333ff605f92ffcd9b6d0703c7b2faec0209/com.anydesk.Anydesk.json#L16
https://docs.flatpak.org/en/latest/sandbox-permissions-reference.html
author: kolAflash

body:
> > So flatpak can not be used as controlled side.
> 
> @rustdesk It might be possible to use RustDesk as controlled side via flatpak. At least this is possible for the AnyDesk flatpak (just tested it). https://flathub.org/apps/details/com.anydesk.Anydesk
> 
> So there might be a clue how to achieve this for RustDesk too. https://github.com/flathub/com.anydesk.Anydesk
> 
> Maybe this!? https://github.com/flathub/com.anydesk.Anydesk/blob/16172333ff605f92ffcd9b6d0703c7b2faec0209/com.anydesk.Anydesk.json#L16 https://docs.flatpak.org/en/latest/sandbox-permissions-reference.html

I've tested `Anydesk`, it does not have an daemon service, so it cannot be invoked when the `Anydesk` is closed. `RustDesk` aims to handle this feature, so we need a small and light daemon service to run forever in the background, which gives `RustDesk` ability to automatically open when a new connection is comming. We assume that flatpak does not support daemon service currently due to [this](https://flatpak.org/faq/#Can_Flatpak_be_used_on_servers_too_), it lacks [Service Management](https://snapcraft.io/docs/service-management) that `snap` contains.
author: Kingtous

body:
@Kingtous  
> I've tested `Anydesk`, it does not have an daemon service, so it cannot be invoked when the `Anydesk` is closed. `RustDesk` aims to handle this feature, so we need a small and light daemon service to run forever in the background, which gives `RustDesk` ability to automatically open when a new connection is comming.

Sure, a background daemon is something else.

But in principal running RustDesk via FlatPack should should be possible for the controlled side. It's just necessary that the RustDesk application runs in "foreground" and not only as a daemon.

 

> So flatpak can not be used as controlled side.

Maybe I got @rustdesk comment wrong. I just wanted to say, that it's possible to control a desktop from an application inside FlatPack in general.
author: kolAflash

body: Other projects do this by staying in the tray, which seems to be a viable solution to this problem until daemon support lands on flatpack (if ever).
author: Root-Core

body:
Is there a way to run the nightly flatpak image in the background? For example like `flatpak run com.rustdesk.RustDesk --background`. I was able to create a autostart file doing the following steps. But now every time my computer starts the rustdesk windows pops up.

```
$ nano ~/.config/systemd/user/rustdesk.service

[Unit]
Description=Remote Desktop
StartLimitIntervalSec=500
StartLimitBurst=5

[Service]
ExecStart=flatpak run com.rustdesk.RustDesk
ExecStop=flatpak kill com.rustdesk.RustDesk
Restart=on-failure
RestartSec=5s

[Install]
WantedBy=graphical-session.target

$ systemctl --user enable rustdesk
```

body:
> > The locale code for the catalan language in Spain is ca_ES
> 
> According to  [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Catalonia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Catalonia)
> the ISO 3166 code is ES-CT. I used underscore because of naming consistency. 

That code is for the name of the region, not the language.
https://www.localeplanet.com/icu/ca-ES/index.html

ca_ES is the proper code for the locale. Compare with pt_PT (already in the repo) and pt_BR for example. These distinguish the varieties of Portuguese language spoken in Portugal and Brazil, respectively.

body:
This scroll problem seems to a universal problem when using **track pad**, unfortunately I don't have a good device for windows develop, so I can't fix this problem for windows currently. But this patch is enough for fix mac itself.

Here is all test cases

- [x] Mac control mac (mouse, track pad)
- [x] Ios control mac (gesture) 
- [x] Win control mac (mouse)
- [x] Mac control win (mouse)

- [ ] Win control mac (track pad)
- [ ] Mac control win (track pad)

So there will need a follow patch to distinguish track pad input from mouse input, and send track pad input on win (linux may also needs support).

body: Today I got the "Connection error" "An existing connection was forcibly closed by the remote host. (os error 10054)" on an already established session that was in viewer mode.  The client did not automatically try to reconnect the session, it had to be done manually. Is there any way to handle this reconnect retrying automatically?
author: fragtion

body: @fragtion It happened when you try to start a connection and the messagebox prompted relay?
author: 21pages

body:
> @fragtion It happened when you try to start a connection and the messagebox prompted relay?

No, connection was already established for several days. Something caused an interruption (could have been the internet connection) and then the error popped up.  But when this specific error occurs, the client does not try to reconnect automatically. it would be nice to change this behavior if required, so that if connection is terminated for any reason the session will try to re-establish automatically

Client is the win32 sciter version in this case but I think the same applies for the other builds
author: fragtion

body: Upload a video please if you want us consider your proposal seriously. It will help us understand your scenario.
author: rustdesk

body: In the current program, when encountering error code 10054, if it is a direct connection and no data is received, it will attempt to relay reconnect. If not, error code 10054 will not trigger a reconnection attempt.

body:
> In the current program, when encountering error code 10054, if it is a direct connection and no data is received, it will attempt to relay reconnect. If not, error code 10054 will not trigger a reconnection attempt.

For the record:

- It was a direct IP connection.
- It did not attempt to reconnect in any way even after several minutes of just sitting there with the error dialog overlayed over the last view/frame of the remote desktop.
- I manually acknowledged the error and reconnected myself, causing it to reconnect very quickly and has been stable again since.
author: fragtion

> different from just sharing a screenshot of the error window

Big difference.
author: rustdesk

body:
If I unplug the LAN cable while a session is in progress, after some moments I get a similar message:
![image](https://github.com/rustdesk/rustdesk/assets/2876889/613cfca5-2056-4a15-95c9-a8f84b6bbc63)

I plugged the LAN cable back in, and have waited 5 minutes. The error message is still on the screen and there is no evidence that the client has automatically tried to re-establish the session. Indeed, the error message stating "Please try later" suggests that the client won't attempt to automatically reconnect (at least not further than it already has) but that I must do it manually myself

So the original request applies here too (support for a feature that allows _infinite_ automatic reconnection retries when such errors occur)

If it can be fixed for this error message (as pictured) then I will try to reproduce it for the 10054 error again to see if the fix works for that too. No use wasting time hunting down the 10054 and making a video if it is easy to reproduce a failed reconnect scenario by simply unplugging the lan cable and seeing the above error which basically has the same effect

BTW, I note you said "if it is a direct connection and no data is received, it will attempt to relay reconnect".  Personally I don't think this ideal either.  If the original connection was via Direct IP, it should always reconnect via Direct IP.  If that fails, the user should set up a separate host definition for relay connection and manually use that if needed. Otherwise, the reconnect behavior type should be configurable somewhere (and in that case relay fallback for failed direct connections could be one of the available options). Some users consider relayed connection a privacy concern so this type of operation occurring invisibly behind the scenes is questionable, even if it was designed that way for a positive user experience

Thnx to anyone who cares to put time into investigating/improving this. It's not essential or high priority, but would definitely be a welcome improvement

> @rustdesk I could check by building the project, but not in the current 1.1.9 release version, nor in the nightly 1.2.0 version, I can't get the RustDesk icon to appear in the tray (menu bar) on macOS. When should it appear? On Windows, it appears after installation as a Windows Service, but on macOS, the application is immediately considered installed after moving it to `/Applications`, which I did.
> 
> сс @Kingtous
> 
> P.S. Looks like Flutter [doesn't support system tray out of the box](https://stackoverflow.com/q/64305453/11336855), third party libraries are required.

on macOS, we should find a way of creating a `NSImage` object from a svg file, which accepts by macOS SDK. i.e., with a `NSImage`, we can easily create a tray icon from the svg. Maybe a `SVGKit` macos plugin (load and use `SVGKExporterNSImage` to get a OSX `NSImage`) or something needs to be added. And we need to render it first to NSImage ahead and then set the tray icon. I dont know whether the refactor is necessary, considering the more complexity of setting a macOS tray icon, and also the time cost of rendering svg.

> I could check by building the project, but not in the current 1.1.9 release version, nor in the nightly 1.2.0 version, I can't get the RustDesk icon to appear in the tray (menu bar) on macOS. 

Also, I've found [native svg support has been added in XCode 12](https://developer.apple.com/forums/thread/113015), which supports macOS 10.15+. But a valid NSImage seems cannot directly be loaded from a svg file.

body:
> 2\. For connections. I think it is necessary to check remote peer OS . If MacOS --> MacOS, do not swap key.

If both computers use swap settings, there is no effect because key input is swapped  once on the client,  and the server swapped it again.
author: sj6219

body:
> > 2. For connections. I think it is necessary to check remote peer OS . If MacOS --> MacOS, do not swap key.
> 
> If both computers use swap settings, there is no effect because key input is swapped once on the client, and the server swapped it again.

Yes. But it will go strange if only one side use swap setting, which is possible.
Check if both MacOS and ignore swapping is what we can control, and the result will always be expected.
author: fufesou

body:
I think it may be better to allow swap key on the local side, whether it's MacOS or not.
The remote side may not swap keys.

It will be more convenient if the control side can decide whether or not to swap keys.
author: fufesou

body: To do so, We need to save settings for each server the client access. It will take time to change it because I am a beginner in flutter language.
author: sj6219

body:
> To do so, We need to save settings for each server the client access. It will take time to change it because I am a beginner in flutter language.

Maybe no need to save settings for each connection.

The setting can be what you've designed. It is an option that should be set once and applied to each connection.
1. It should checked and applied when each connection is established.
2. It should be applied to each establised connection when the setting is changed.

BTW, "Allow swap key" option may be easier to find on the remote page(remote menu). Like the [`kb_layout_type`](https://github.com/fufesou/rustdesk/blob/85dc3b0e586fa8ca35ac729fb03fd71019f9772a/libs/hbb_common/src/config.rs#L1074) option.

body:
> I did have this idea.

Immediate expanding on hover is a step in the wrong direction imo. The auto expand behavior happens currently in the nightly as I explained in a bug, and causes misclicks on other options.

A suggestion I would make would be:

Show all monitors as individual buttons. Require explicit clicks on menu options.

Add an additional button that you can click on that expands a menu showing the physical location of the connected monitors. This would satisfy the ask of this bug without compromising the new menu bar theme, and retain the feature found in 1.9.x

Pardon my crude mspaint skills:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/8310293/224356620-2ea16c16-10a5-4fda-9dd1-68f4fc450b28.png)

body:
> * unclear state of addressbook, can't login, can't work on it
> * unclear state of menu items "add/remove addressbook" in other lists than AddressbookCard
> * In addressbook menu, changing "Remove" to " Remove from addressbook" may be better …

Email me and I'll send you a server, it's under development but available

> * ambigous meaning of "remove". Remove from current list (hide from view?) or delete from database.

It's a safe choice to keep deleting the local database.
> * a disabled "MenuEntryButton" could helpful in some cases.

Agree
> * what happen to a discovered peer on "remove"? Will they no longer detected? if (isLAN) { TODO } any todos?

You found the blind spot, it will reappear.
And its "Remove" doesn't work now, it shoud means remove outdated peers.
> * card overlay for "favorite" and " addressbook" state would be nice

Different card style in recent card? A consistent style may look cleaner
> * looking for something like "(rx) FavoritesList" or "(rx) Peer.IsFavorite" for smoother working with its fav state

Yes, currently relies on local database reads
> * is "WOL" too nerdisch? should the button text more common user friendly? "Wake up (WOL)" or "Start remote machine (WOL)" or similar

Agree, most people don't know what it means, it means allowing this device to be discovered on the local network, but need to be shorter
> * some sort of "progress" indicator for WOL. Check "isOnline" for a while in a loop and give feedback. Or inform user "waiting a while".

Discovery is fast, results are dynamic, and the progress is difficult to judge ;)

author: rustdesk

body:
Please consider the layout shift. 

The eye is focused on one specific item in the list and suddenly there is a move to the left. Focus lost unexpected. This is irritating. Users are "surprised" when "suddenly" something happens that "they didn't expect".

How long will this two column view be visible before the first file transfer is triggered? Afterwards the third column view is there anyhow.

Adding a toggle is a great idea, so the user has control over whether the transfers stack is visible or not. The layout shift is then also expected.

My suggestion is set the toggle default to "true" and replaceing this "empty" with a pleasing message.

In a further cycle the toggle could be turned into some sort of "notification" area, with loading indicator, short stats, expands (as overflow) and reveal the transfers stack, with option "make it sticky".

If you are satisfied with it, i am too. You have done a great job!

All concerns i have spotted out are about readability, commnunication, accessibility, consistency, usability, etc. These have been resolved from my perspective.

Having a two-column view by default is an absolutely valid wish, i can see it on the horizon, but it's the horizon and it will take a little more time to implement this in a sophisticated way.

You have already expressed that your time is limited. The fact that you still haven't stopped continuing here shows a lot about you. You call it a "big step", i would call it a giant leap and a great finish for this current cycle of designing the file transfer view.


Thats what i think.

```
Line 12  ("Start Service", "Vermittlungsdienst starten"),
                            ^^^^^^^^^^ [1] ^^^^^^^^^^^

Line 273 ("CLOSE", "SCHLIEẞEN"),
Line 274 ("OPEN", "ÖFFNEN"),    
                   ^[2]^^^                       
Line 293 ("android_start_service_tip", "Tippen Sie auf \"Dienst aktivieren\" oder aktivieren Sie die Berechtigung \"Bildschirmaufnahme\", um den Bildschirmfreigabedienst zu starten."),
                                                         ^^^^^^ [1] ^^^^^^^      ^^^ [2] ^^^

```

- How do you keep care of the context?
- Do you have problems with figuring out which text is placed where in the UI?
- Could it help grouping translatables more together?
- What do you think of placeholders "Tippen Sie auf "`%button.start_service.text%`" oder …"
- Do you have an idea of what would be necessary for you to produce better quality and more contextual translations?

Danke!

author: grummbeer

body:
@ grummbeer
> * How do you keep care of the context?
> * Do you have problems with figuring out which text is placed where in the UI?
> * Could it help grouping translatables more together?
> * What do you think of placeholders "Tippen Sie auf "`%button.start_service.text%`" oder …"
> * Do you have an idea of what would be necessary for you to produce better quality and more contextual translations?

A wonderful idea to see where the text is placed is the tool TrlUtil for Keepass (https://keepass.info/translations_devinfo.html).
In the 'Dialogs' tab, clicking on an entry shows the translated text in an extra window (behind it). This opens up the context and the text length can be judged well.

Placeholders are a very good idea for consistent translation.

For the explanation of the context a comment in Template.rs would be helpful, e.g. at "Elevate".

author: rustdesk

body:
> Can we keep the "cancel" button outlined? Let's see below two buttons on GitHub.

Agree, github do it right. 

There are buttons on the right side that "confirm" the purpose of the dialog. "apply", "OK", "do", "go". Lefthand are buttons that represent the "safe harbor" with "cancel", "clear", "don't", "don't go". The colors should reflect the consequences.


I must think on it, is this a massive overhaul what affected the whole app, dark/light desktop/mobile and technical concepts as well? And some side effects, depending on the theming, will appear or are already introduced (so far).  


- very good: Indroducing theming.
- good: more and more components covered by theme and goes unified. 
- semi good: the theme changes reveals app wide the affected Elements, good to know but needs to be fixed now. 
- very bad: General theming is not implemented well at all (yet).
- good: Confirm destructive action (peer delete)
- good: Checkboxes are a bit "new rounded" and blue on dark, the checkColor can now be white again (perfect contrast, same color label).
- consider on: How to go rounded and flat but keep usability and accessibility (best UX, just rounded)
    - Flat in flat makes it hard to detect what or where an action is required or possible (button, input field) and where not. The typical design of a button/input does not have this problem (well known concept).
    - Color in color (weak contrasted) makes it hard to distinguish the elements.
- consider on: Beware of the differences between mobile and desktop and the shared parts. e.g. "CustomAlertDialog", "ThemeData".
- Buttons/actions in CustomDialog are bypassed. Why not create a "DesktopDialog" and keep the not fitting CustomDialog for the mobile one. In general i vote for stricter separation of mobile and desktop, components does not fit for both in every case. this Dialog is a good sample. Or make the shared components smaller.
- The approach looks twisted. Its bottom up "Component Dialog > App (theme)" and not top down "App (theme) > Compontent Dialog"

not a easy task … but how about changing focus from specific Component "Dialog" to the App theming first?

1. Implement a stable theming, create the foundation.
   - if possible keep the current look, just covered by the theme.
   - keep the difference between mobile/desktop 
      - create Theme extensions if required, eg. "mobileDark" or "mobileLight" (or similar no idea)
   - split or create dedicated components for mobile/desktop if required
   - organize the colors, currently is wilderness

2. Redesign Components, relies on a well implemented Theme.
   - predictable
   - no surprises
   - no unwanted side effects ("… oops, i wasn't aware of that")
   - easy in the best case, one change for all (per platform)

3. Envolve
   - add new features (e.g. "confirm delete")
   - you need a button for your feature? no problem, there is a quite nice collection of presets for your purpose.

body:
@NicKoehler a bit a pitty, but the "modern dialogs" came to fast and do not fit in all situations, but we can't have different dialogs across the application, so i think reverting the rounded is the best for now.

The goal is not doing the best visual styling (i can't at all, i am not a designer), the goal is making a equal look by creating a foundation in the hope afterwards it will be possible forcing **all** dialogs to the same look by changing just a few vars.

All input fields/dialogs looks like this (ignore coloring for now, will be adjusted):

### Mobile

- Step back, close to the defaults, looks clean and pure, not buggy, as long as a better style fit for **all** 
- Inner padding will be adjusted.

| | | colors still too dark|compare/lineage default|
|--|--|--|--|

### desktop

#### Buttons
   
- Adjust inner padding for better height/width radio
- Unify inner padding for outlined and icon vs. no icon.
- All buttons an desktop will have this look then

|now |after, equal spacing (ignore coloring)|
|-- |-- |
|![buttons-unequal-paddings](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/67791701/225740520-b61b9251-637b-4cb8-a007-142dcfddd460.png)|![buttons-after](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/67791701/225741529-90542026-d59d-4941-9107-d32e466f43f0.png)|

body:
@mehdi-song I am curious, maybe you can help me understand the translations better.

### RTL

The headline of the "RDP Settings" (home | peers > Windows > three dot > RDP (pencil)) is a composition of "RDP + [Settings]", what results in the headline "RDP تنظیمات".

If and how does that affect your ~~LTR~~ RTL flow?

If you could translate the headline in whole, what would it be?

("RDP Settings", "?"),

### context 

Do you always know where the translated text is show in the app? In which context it is used?

thanks.
author: grummbeer

body:
@mehdi-song a sample from german translation.

```
("Elevate", "Erheben"),
```

This is a direct word-to-word translation from the dictionary, but in this context it is completely wrong, because "elevate" does not mean "a mountain evelates from the ground" or "a button elevates from background" (which is the German "Erheben"). It is used in the context of "grant access" or "elevate user rights", what needs a totally other translation.

I suspect that translators may not know where a text is used, and therefore simply translate what is seen on the left.

What do you think, is something like this possible in fa.rs?
author: grummbeer

body:
Hi @grummbeer 
Yes that right , some times I tested the Pre-release  to see what is wrong in translate and then correct them.
In translate `relay_hint_tip` I saw `en.rs` to find the text message but there is a problem in LTR RTL flow, `("Enable TCP Tunneling", "را فعال کنید TCP تانل")` if I write `TCP Tunneling را فعال کنید` the RTL not correct and I must write `را فعال کنید TCP تانل`.
In little text it's not hard to translate but in long text it is a problem

>  I saw en.rs to find the text message

But it holds not all parts, but maybe should. 

Do you have an idea of what would be necessary for you to produce better quality and more contextual translations?

body:
@Mr-Update 

```
" Wird installiert …"),
 ^ space
```

Beside that … if you can find time, it would be nice to hear your thoughts on this:

`installiere …` vs. `Wird installiert …`

This translation refer to a good point. Its about humen <> machine communication

Q: Computer what **do you doing**?
A: installing …

vs.

Q: Computer what **is the current state**?
A: Installation in progress.

Do you see the difference and do you think there are more places that mix up this kind of Q and A?

Which kind would you prefer?
author: grummbeer

body:
@grummbeer
> `installiere …` vs. `Wird installiert …`

- In the conjugation table you can see that all four cases of "installiere" are wrong for the translation (https://www.duden.de/konjugation/installieren):

```
- Indikativ, Präsens, Aktiv
ich installiere (mich)

- Konjunktiv I, Präsens, Aktiv
ich installiere (mich)
er/sie/es installiere (sich)

- Imperativ
2. Person Singular [du] installiere (dich)!
```

- That's why I think a good translation is (https://de.wiktionary.org/wiki/Flexion:installieren):

```
- Indikativ, Präsens, Vorgangspassiv
Er/sie/es wird installiert ...
```

- Very nice also to read here (https://wiki.gnome.org/de/UebersetzungsRichtlinien#line-17):

![BIld5](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/37781396/223551469-9607f631-779e-4cc0-9887-b8d77090ba10.png)
author: Mr-Update

body:
Thanks for the links.

> Wird installiert …

Yes, this is perfect. Thanks for the correction, was a misunderstanding, I had not the slightest doubt on it.

I wanted spot to another point regarding the Q&A style/communication.

Sample: 

```
("Listening ...", "Lauschen …"),
```

Headline RDP View
|||
|--|--|
|![rdp](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/67791701/223556986-2fc64a76-0fff-4917-ac1d-01267b35e5d5.png)|![rdp-de](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/67791701/223557668-54258527-b369-47c7-9a55-71ea26673a46.png)|

Same again.

Q: Computer what are you doing?
A: (I am) listening …

vs.

Q: Computer what is the current state?
A: Waiting for the connection.

Should we answer the unasked question "What are you doing?" or "What is the current status/what is happening right now?"

What do you think would be a good style that meets the general understanding of "the common" users? The massive crowd of common users that came in with the last home office wave. Do they know what a computer means with "listening …" or what an "tunnel" is?

I am pretty sure the my-wife-test (I really mean my wife) will fail on "Lauschen" or "Tunnel". The first one makes her think of the bedtime story, the second one makes her think of turning on the light.

Thanks.
author: grummbeer

body:

> ("Listening ...", "Lauschen …"),

"Lauschen …" is ok.
Alternatives would be:

body:
Danke mal für die Rückmeldung. Ich muss mich noch mehr mit der Sache auseinandersetzen. Bisher habe ich nur das Gefühl, dass die Kommnuikation mangelhaft ist und irgendwie verbessert werden müsste. Es gibt einige Stellen, die irgendwie nicht konsistent, mehrdeutig oder gar wiedersprüchlich erscheinen. Ich muss noch mehr Eindrücke/Beispiele sammeln.

Bezüglich dem "Lauschen" denke ich, dass es nicht die angemessene Formulierung ist, um dem Anwender verständlich zu machen, was dies überhaupt bedeutet. Ich stelle mir vor, dass die Kommunikation so verständlich und beschreibend als möglich sein sollte, damit keine Rätsel aufgegeben oder unverständliches "Fachchinesisch" Fragezeichen hinterlässt.

So wie es aussieht hast Du mehr Erfahrung bei dem Thema wie ich. Wäre cool, wenn Du mithelfen könntest die bestmögliche Kommunikation, zumindest für den deuschsprachigen Raum, zu gewährleisten.

Du machst das gut, einfach weitermachen. Danke.

body:
> libxkbcommon-x11-dev

xkbcommon is a library for handling of keyboard descriptions, including loading them from disk, parsing them and handling their state. It's mainly meant for client toolkits, window systems, and other system applications;

Currently that includes:

- [Wayland](https://wayland.freedesktop.org/)
- [kmscon](https://github.com/dvdhrm/kmscon/wiki/KMSCON)
- [GTK](https://www.gtk.org/)
- [Qt](https://www.qt.io/)
- [Clutter](https://live.gnome.org/Clutter)
- [KWin](https://userbase.kde.org/KWin)

---

xkbcommon supports x11, wayland, it can better handle the simulation on the Linux side.

sorry, bad testing. Only the file connection …

### Linux > Android 

- 🗹 file connection
- 🗹 screen connection

### Android > Linux

- 🗹 file connection
- ❌ screen connection

Two observations

1. Screen connection

When trying a screen connection, after "accept" by peer

```
E/flutter ( 1718): [ERROR:flutter/runtime/dart_vm_initializer.cc(41)] Unhandled Exception:
 "RxBool" not found. You need to call "Get.put(RxBool())" or "Get.lazyPut(()=>RxBool())"

E/flutter ( 1718): #0      GetInstance.find get_instance.dart:305
E/flutter ( 1718): #1      Inst.find extension_instance.dart:69
E/flutter ( 1718): #2      ShowRemoteCursorState.find shared_state.dart:146
E/flutter ( 1718): #3      FfiModel.setViewOnly model.dart:541
E/flutter ( 1718): #4      FfiModel.handlePeerInfo model.dart:452
E/flutter ( 1718): <asynchronous suspension>
```

2. Screen connection after file connection
 
When establish a file connection first, which works, close and try a screen connection afterwards, the point of exception changed

```
E/flutter ( 1718): [ERROR:flutter/runtime/dart_vm_initializer.cc(41)] Unhandled Exception: Null check operator used on a null value
E/flutter ( 1718): #0      ImageModel.onRgba   model.dart:567
E/flutter ( 1718): #1      FFI.start.<anonymous closure> model.dart:1603
E/flutter ( 1718): <asynchronous suspension>

I/flutter ( 1718): Failed to openDirectory : Failed to read dir, timeout
```

`if (_waitForImage[id]!)`

If an exception is wanted here than it's OK, if not, than according to https://dart.dev/language/operators#other-operators, it seems that the "Null assertion operator" is not the best choise for such a condition. Lost connection to device.

>> 2, Screen connection after file connection

> Crashed image and error?

Strange, this behaviour is depend on a file connection, which established and closed before. Even after you patch.

Connection keep established.

body:
Terrible, it would be better if I did not write this issue at all. Now even more broken than before.

I'm using a custom Colemak layout on top of QWERTY virtual keys so that when using hotkeys, they are interpreted as QWERTY-based. This allows me to use custom layouts from other languages while maintaining the same way of using hotkeys. I am using Win2Win connection in RustDesk. The remote machine does not have my layouts. I need to write on a remote machine on the Сolemak layout and use QWERTY-based hotkeys. The first implementation of the key translation mode worked perfectly for this task.

![2023-03-31_212421](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/80201571/229200391-1a559763-62e1-482c-a7d5-906c08ddf0f6.png)

author: fufesou

body:
@cypherpunks03 I've tried your layout. 

For hotkeys in "Translate Mode", there are two ways to simulate:

1. Pass virtual keycode  
    Friendly to the original input layouts. But for custom input methods, the hotkeys keep original for the virtual key never changes.
2. Pass unicode characters  
    Friendly to the custom input layouts. But only for shortcut keys of ascii code. Cann't simulate some hotkeys that are not ascii.


I've also tested pressing 'e' and Ctrl + 'e', results:
```
Local:    'f', Ctrl + VK_E
TeamView: 'f', Ctrl + VK_E
AnyDesk:  'f', Ctrl + VK_F
RustDesk: 'f', Ctrl + VK_E
```


https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/13586388/229271657-79224ce9-832e-459a-a53e-585081306d62.mp4

body:
> Input data callback maybe faster than output data callback, which cause increased delay.

So we can use this https://github.com/rustdesk/rustdesk/issues/534#issuecomment-1144564127 method. By adjusting output sample_rate (higher a little than real data sample_rate), or find out why output callback can not follow up (we can write a simple demo to see if it can catch up to see if our wrong usage or sdk problem).
author: rustdesk

body: The network audio data flow can not be synced with playback, one is not stable, the other is fixed speed (playback rate). To avoid clipping, have to find way to dynamically change playback rate. Sadly, we can not find in cpal so far, let do it in the future. We tried to resample to lower rate data, but it causes big noise.

author: ghost

body:
I'm not at home now. 
For translate mode, letter simulation:
1. An pressing event occurs
2. The local side gets the letter
3.  Transmits the letter
4. The remote side simulate the letter(unicode). Key down and key up.

When an releasing event occurs, the remote side just simulate the position code(scancode). Because it is safe to release multiple times. And there're no intersections between letter keys and other keys.

body:
<!---
Got it perfectly working on:

- **Arch Linux**
- **X11** display server
- `xf86-video-dummy` driver
- **i3** window manager
- **lightdm** display manager

For about 6 months now. Either on public rustdesk servers or self-hosted.

So being on `gnome` and/or Ubuntu is absolutely unneeded. Basically any Linux system on X11 with the mentioned driver will work, provided nothings goes in the way. That should make every Linux user happy 😄🚀 ! 

PS: While teamviewer doesn't even work on this setup, it doesn't detect any display as soon as I unlock the session on the LightDM screen. This really feels great ! Thanks @rustdesk !
-->
author: adamency

body:
I was able to get this working for Ubuntu 22.04.03 LTS by using the following xorg config (inserted at `/usr/share/X11/xorg.conf.d/xorg.conf`).

I had to create the config file as it was missing, I hope this helps someone else.

This is for a 1920x1080 display via X11/xorg, 

```
Section "Device"
  Identifier "Configured Video Device"
  Driver "dummy"
  VideoRam 16384
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
  Identifier "Configured Monitor"
  HorizSync 5.0 - 1000.0
  VertRefresh 5.0 - 1000.0
  ModeLine "1920x1080" 23.53 1920 1952 2040 2072 1080 1106 1108 1135
EndSection

Section "Screen"
  Identifier "Default Screen"
  Monitor "Configured Monitor"
  Device "Configured Video Device"
  DefaultDepth 24
  SubSection "Display"
    Depth 24
    Modes "1920x1080"
  EndSubSection
EndSection
```

For further details regarding Xorg configuration please see: https://wiki.archlinux.org/title/xorg

body:
@rustdesk

For me your consideration about "efficient" could be read in other way as "less job as possible".

I'm quite surprise that consider my professional request about male/female strings for not English language an "happy" consideration.

It means that you want to ignore that your application is not only English but is multl language.

English strings are not male/female.
Other language strings can be male/female.

Use "Default langauge" that it's specific it's better also if you have only English.

You have a strange and quite caotic approach about translation.

As the tips that is not include as complete strings (but only variable name) in template.rs and are instead available in en.rs.

Then to create/update a language the translator have to check two files (template.rs and en.rs).
What's the reason of this caotic and complicated approach.

Simple: probably less job to do.
For me is not professional and is not good for translator and for future improvements.

Please switch your mind to do a clear multi language approach and simple and clear situation about translation.

rename template.rs as _template,rs to see in top of list (and not in middle of other languages).
merge the strings of en.rs in template.rs and change in source code xxx_xxx_tip with full text strings.

Then the _template.rs will be a real template useful for translator to check/update language.

Thanks.

author: lpcy

body:
Sorry for late. I've read the code.

RustDesk only support one of physical mouse or touch pointer in one session.

https://github.com/rustdesk/rustdesk/blob/67d0ff4fe1bd46ec1c655481127394098b7d0b7b/flutter/lib/mobile/pages/remote_page.dart#L273

`events` in `getBodyForMobile()` are handled by the place you've modified.  
`events` in `getBodyForMobileWithGesture` are handled by `updatePan`.

https://github.com/rustdesk/rustdesk/blob/357028b222d8973add39f07a333dad2b92c33b10/flutter/lib/mobile/pages/remote_page.dart#L463

If you've ever connected a physical mouse or triggered `PointerHoverEvent.kind` as `PointerHoverEvent.kind.mouse`, RustDesk will set `isPhysicalMouse` to be `true`, thus it will not handle event on touch event.

Did you connected a physical mouse after connecting to the peer?

I'm confused about your tablet environment. :joy:

body:
There's a side effect that the resolution will be reset to original when plug in virtual display.

`dummy_display_side_max_size` is set to 1200.

I mannually set the remote resolution to `800x600`.
The resolution is set `2160x1440` automatically when the connection is established and the virtual display is plug in.
There's no record of resolution restore.

I'll try to find the reason.
author: fufesou

body:
It works fine.

The resolution of display 0 is set to `2160x1440` when the virtual display is plug in.

Because the setting of two displays is set by previous operations.
When new monitor is plug in, the setting is applied.

body:
@rustdesk Thanks, I've rebased the branch.

Fwiw: from my own testing the perfect bitrate is around _screen width * screen height * fps_ bit/s (around 40 times less than if you'd send every frame as bitmap). While the fps seem to be mostly CPU bound.
For me that's about a bitrate of 1000%, i.e. ~74 mbit/s for 20 fps with a heavy animation running on a 2560x1440 screen.

I'll try the build with the modified qos over the next few days.
author: bwoebi

body:
@rustdesk I like the qos changes, I had noticeably less spurious stutter, in particular with increased bitrate. Thanks for the improvements!

However, with the standard bitrate heavily animated content would still just degrade too much. (Even though it's better than before.)

            onPressed: () => widget.setMinimize(),
            child: Tooltip(
              message: translate('Minimize'),
              child: Icon(
                Icons.remove,
                size: iconSize,
              ),
            ),
          )
```

body: can `/usr/share/rustdesk/files/rustdesk.png` be found automatically and magically?
author: rustdesk

body:
> can `/usr/share/rustdesk/files/rustdesk.png` be found automatically and magically?

The correct path for icon is `/usr/share/icons/hicolor/256x256/apps/rustdesk.png`. If the icon is placed in this directory then it will be used only by name. Every distro package should place it there because it's in the [XDG specs](https://specifications.freedesktop.org/icon-theme-spec/icon-theme-spec-latest.html#install_icons).

E.g. Arch package [does that correctly](https://aur.archlinux.org/cgit/aur.git/tree/PKGBUILD?h=rustdesk-nightly).

body:
> Will this cut off the capability to common formats like `CF_TEXT`?

No.

There're two clipboard listeners for now, crate [`arboard`](https://crates.io/crates/arboard)  and the win file clipboard.

The win file clipboard only handles files for simplify. Even `CF_TEXT` can also be handled.

author: fufesou

body:
Hi @fufesou, most errors we encounter in copy & paste file are clipboard timeout. I had once written a dead lock bug with dependency's wait function, and by using time limited API the bug was then solved.

Sadly, when the load of Xorg gets higher, it can hardly return the mime data we requested in time, leading to a timeout expection cutting off file list synchronization. This will cause the symptom of no updating peer's clipboard, hence you cannot paste data on peer's file manager.

The problem should now be solved since I make the client used for file copying & pasting wait forever. It will keep waiting unless the file list data is available.

The service should better be root.
We need the root permission to do something, like uinput.

The server process on Wayland is of user permission. It is started by the service process.

https://github.com/rustdesk/rustdesk/blob/master/src/platform/linux.rs#L209

body:
Thanks @fufesou 
This has to be something related to arboard. Maybe, as you mentioned above, setting up `WAYLAND_DISPLAY` can help. I will try it out.
Until now, I was working on implementing the clipboard portal (from xdg-desktop-portal) on Arch. However, I found that since this portal has been very recently released, it is not available on previous versions of distros like Ubuntu and Debian (only available on the latest versions). This would not be good for many users. So I ditched the clipboard portal now and I'm trying the data control protocol. Maybe, just extending this PR changes by improving compatibility will help.

The data control protocol is supported by kwin and sway compositors, and gnome is already covered by XWayland. So, we will have clipboard support on all these three compositors. I think that covers the majority of Wayland users.

Ok so `WAYLAND_DISPLAY` env is most likely the solution.
It is not specifically needed by arboard(except for a check) but it is required by arboard->wl-clipboard-rs->[wayland-client::Connection::connect_to_env() ](https://docs.rs/wayland-client/latest/src/wayland_client/conn.rs.html#49-94) to make a connection to the wayland compositor.

@fufesou As you mentioned above https://github.com/rustdesk/rustdesk/pull/5900#issuecomment-1752707804 I have added the env for `WAYLAND_DISPLAY` but the [arboard code](https://github.com/1Password/arboard/blob/1b8df75ee234dc8ae13785528bd34e4cb1aba8c3/src/platform/linux/mod.rs#L81) is still logging empty/none value for it, clipboard is not working.

Can you please check if I set it correctly? https://github.com/rustdesk/rustdesk/compare/master...sahilyeole:rustdesk:feat/wayland_clipboard
My log for envs vector:
```[("DISPLAY", ":1"), ("XAUTHORITY", "/run/user/1000/xauth_sbpBTa"), ("WAYLAND_DISPLAY", "wayland-0")]```

---
Edit: I manually set env in arboard code and now clipboard is working, so confirmed `WAYLAND_DISPLAY` env is the solution.

But envs received by arboard code are:
```

body:
I found a new way to do this. Accessibility service has a method called getInputMethod, we could then send key event directly to it.This could solve previous problem.
> Input events will have a side effect that will set the cursor to the end. So the arrow control doesn't work, the cursor always moves to the end.

Old way can only set text and text selection separately, if application doesn't do some special text handling, this is fine.But if app would change text after text set, then our text selection data would be wrong. So I ignored text selection data for correctness. In this new way I leave this to system to handle, arrow key works fine.
> The input does not work on the input box on the web page. Tested chrome browser.

This is because findFocus gave a wrong input widget, send event to IME also fix this.

======================== update =============================

Maybe you need to use the `scancode` in the `KeyEvent` when using the "Map mode".

https://developer.android.com/reference/android/view/KeyEvent#KeyEvent

"Map mode" means using the "position code" (scancode) to control the key inputs.

With "Map mode", people can control the peer as the physical keyboard is connected directly to the peer.

body: I am trying to implement Map mode these days.But there are two problems. First we can only send a keycode that matches US keyboard layout, Android keycode only has those keys. Set scancode of a keyevent is ineffective base on my experiments. Second I only found ways to send key events to application, not send to input method. On Android a hardware keyboard event is sent to input method, then the input method would figure out correct text to insert into application. I failed to find a way to do the same thing. However I have not tested what text would be inserted if the OS language matches input method, because my android device couldn't do that. If that could work, we could add map mode back.

body:
@fufesou Hi!
I fixed the issue of not being able to input characters. It's a issue around findFocus method. Please take a look.

About the ctrl-a shortcut, It seems It's not an Android system shortcut, It's done by AnyDesk itself. I tried that shortcut directly in simulator, nothing happened. I don't know if we should do the same thing, implementing a shortcut collection that system doesn't have.

The cursor is a tricky problem, because we can't set text and selection at the same time, application might change text after we set text, if they did, the cursor position would be wrong. AnyDesk has the same problem, you could reproduce this bug by texting in RustDesk address field while using AnyDesk. The cursor would be in middle of the text not the end, you will need use arrow key to navigate to the end every time you input a letter. I think this is more annoying than you can't move the cursor with arrow key. So I choose to not update cursor location.

The mouse issue is another problem, sometimes it works. I don't know what's going on right now, but we could track it in a new issue maybe?
author: mcfans

body:
> About the ctrl-a shortcut, It seems It's not an Android system shortcut, It's done by AnyDesk itself. I tried that shortcut directly in simulator, nothing happened. I don't know if we should do the same thing, implementing a shortcut collection that system doesn't have.

I think the `Ctrl+` shortcuts are supported by Android. The emulator does not handle the `Ctrl +` shortcut correctly.

I've tried to connect a physical mouse and keyboard to Android. The shortcut experience is very good.

> The cursor is a tricky problem, because we can't set text and selection at the same time, application might change text after we set text, if they did, the cursor position would be wrong. AnyDesk has the same problem, you could reproduce this bug by texting in RustDesk address field while using AnyDesk. 

I'll try, but the address field is a one line editor.

Maybe you can also try a notepad app?


> 2. Keys of "Arrows" "Home/End" "PageUp/PageDown" work fine.
> 3. Shift + "Arrow" work fine.

Not only the "Arrow" keys, but also the other keys work fine. So I think AnyDesk can handle these non-letter keys well.

For API level 34, I am using arm64 apk on x86 emulator. It’s okay for Android 11 and above. For API level 28, I am using arm64 apk on arm64 emulator. I have a M2 MacBook, it’s arm64, so it’s fine. You probably need `—split-per-abi` when running `flutter build`. That will build an apk that contains arm64 only. Without this flag, output apk seems have 3 architecture, dyld trying to open .so in x86 directory, but nothing in there.

body:
These are the patches I added to fdroid build. There are also two other issues I'd like to have fixed:

* The versionCode in android gradle does not increment with time, preventing successful update auto-detection in fdroid. I worked around by setting external CI project https://gitlab.com/basilgello/rustdesk-fdroid-versioner but properly it should be solved by adding the step to on-tag CI pipeline to generate rustdesk-version.txt inside build tree or in release.

* I added android-x86 and android-x64 builds into f-droid because emulators can use only them. Flutter, however, supports building x86 only with jit_release flutter engine which I build from sources. I can set separate CI task for flutter engine build if you are OK adding these architectures.
author: basilgello

body:
@fufesou build fails because https://github.com/Kingtous/rustdesk_thirdparty_lib lack ndk_compat from vcpkg package cpu-features that I added today to address crashes on android arm64 emulators. In flutter/build_android_deps.sh I added that dependency but the @kingtous repo does not use that script.

I also bumped libvpx to 1.13.1 due to CVE-2023-5238 (high).

I'd better run flutter/build_android_deps.sh in CI rather than use third-party repo - on fdroid builder the dependency build time is 6 minutes.

Info: https://github.com/rustdesk/rustdesk/commit/c8f8bfd6f08eba9c0804054fa146fb5500142895#diff-6670c5314a2ea23963709506313c74a348f27bd18831534b16621a44b6771696R58

body:
I tested on Windows:

I use the "shortcut" option on Desktop and I founded out adding `/` at the end of the URL will cause `ID not exist error` with `file-transfer` URL, for example:




|   | |
|----------|-------------|
|`rustdesk://1234567890`|      OK      | 
|`rustdesk://1234567890/`|      OK      | 
|`rustdesk://1234567890/r`|      OK      | 
|   | |
|`rustdesk://file-transfer/1234567890`|      OK      | 
|`rustdesk://file-transfer/1234567890/`|     *ID not exist error*      | 
|`rustdesk://file-transfer/1234567890/r`|      OK      |

```
("Enable blocking user input", "Blockieren von Benutzereingaben zulassen"),
```

"Blockieren … zulassen" klingt uneindeutig?

https://github.com/rustdesk/rustdesk/pull/6303

![permissions-allow-user-input](https://github.com/rustdesk/rustdesk/assets/67791701/af44350f-ac4d-4af9-97b4-9f444e8919b5)


Analog zu den anderen müsste es heißen "Benutzereingaben aktivieren", was sich aber etwas seltsam liest. Es müsste eher lauten wie: "Benutzereingabe erlauben/zulassen" was im rechten Fenster wäre: "Benutzereingabe erlauben/zulassen: ON/OFF".

Das mit dem "zulassen" gib es schon bei "Enable file copy and paste". Wobei das aber klar aus der Reihe fällt.

```
("Enable file copy and paste", "Kopieren und Einfügen von Dateien zulassen"),
```

@21pages Does the left side "enable file transfer" do the same as the "Enable file copy and paste" on the right side? 

![permissions-file-paste](https://github.com/rustdesk/rustdesk/assets/67791701/63ce7a36-4cfb-41f7-b890-4f890a018008)
author: grummbeer

body:
`Block user input` is used to block the input of the controlled side, it's an action name. 
![rustdesk_jYU25rXtfv](https://github.com/rustdesk/rustdesk/assets/14891774/8d7e4966-08ec-496b-aff0-a44cadbb2f89)
`copy and paste` is more accurate for remote connection, so I kept it, it also makes sense to replace it with `file transfer` for better consistency.
author: 21pages

body:
### translations

> `copy and paste` is more accurate for remote connection, so I kept it, it also makes sense to replace it with `file transfer` for better consistency.

"copy and paste" sounds more like "clipboard" (already existing) while "file transfer" match really the transfer of files (which is what is meant here?).

So the action here is replaceing the translation key "Enable file copy and paste"

https://github.com/rustdesk/rustdesk/blob/7bafe142ca513d3247833f31e500911e81e389e6/flutter/lib/desktop/pages/server_page.dart#L631

with "Enable file transfer" and remove the key "Enable file copy and paste" from language files?

https://github.com/rustdesk/rustdesk/blob/7bafe142ca513d3247833f31e500911e81e389e6/src/lang/template.rs#L182

### block user input

> `Block user input` is used to block the input of the controlled side, it's an action name. 

sorry, but i do not understand how this work. This is for the controlled side to block the input from the controlling side? But why has the controlling side this "block user input" action in toolbar?

Linux > Windows

Windows (client) disabled "Enable blocking user input", but 

- controlling side can still do input
- the action "Block user input" is not visible in controlling toolbar (may intended)

![permissions-allow-user-input-2](https://github.com/rustdesk/rustdesk/assets/67791701/a2cf616b-309b-4d6b-acac-7dd497689c15)

When controlled windows enable "Enable blocking user input" then at controlling side the action is visible …
![permissions-allow-user-input-3](https://github.com/rustdesk/rustdesk/assets/67791701/886659ff-2eb2-4184-931c-d6844042a67f)

… but a error is thrown on click.

![permissions-allow-user-input-4](https://github.com/rustdesk/rustdesk/assets/67791701/92d1e8f1-ce3f-4d4b-8728-c2a15227dbed)
author: grummbeer

body:
@Mr-Update what do you think of "Benutzereingaben aktivieren"? For consistency it is the best.

In general I think a permission is not something you can enable/disable, it can be granted or revoked.

> This is for the controlled side to block the input from the controlling side

reverse, require controlled side to be installed

body:
> "copy and paste" sounds more like "clipboard" (already existing) while "file transfer" match really the transfer of files (which is what is meant here?).

`clipboard` is used for text copy/paste, `file copy and paste`  is used for file copy/paste, "file transfer" is ok, we need to make it more clear.

body:
The driver is not recommended.

But if you really want that version, you can:
1. Fork this repo.
2. Checkout a new branch based on https://github.com/rustdesk/rustdesk/tree/9a1fd1aa4f98f8b719a8e57b168cb9cda0356896
3. Push to github.
4. Create a nightly tag, push to github.  like https://github.com/fufesou/rustdesk/tree/nightly
5. Create a nightly pre-release.  like  https://github.com/fufesou/rustdesk/releases/tag/nightly
6. Trigger a new action.  like  https://github.com/fufesou/rustdesk/actions/workflows/flutter-nightly.yml

author: manueldeprada
beelchester

body:
> @manueldeprada The mouse might not work properly because we are using xrandr to get max desktop resolution (addition of all the monitors) and xrandr gives wrong display resolution on gnome fractional scaling (same wrong value as in pipewire stream).
> 
> I'm trying to fix it.

Thanks!! I see there is a mismatch, yes. The good part of only relying on the actual stream size is that now you can also stream windows or multi-monitors and all of this should just work
author: manueldeprada

body:
@manueldeprada https://github.com/rustdesk/rustdesk/pull/6502 will solve the cursor mismatch issue, please test it.

Also, **multi-monitor+gnome(other than ubuntu)+wayland+fractional scaling enabled** will still have cursor mismatch issue, that's because we only get the resolution of current streaming screen. Other than xrandr I could not find any other way to get max desktop resolution on wayland.

**multi-monitor+gnome(other than ubuntu)+wayland+fractional scaling enabled**, this can be a less common case, at this point user can just change scaling to 100% and cursor will work. We may look at this particular case in future if this issue still exists after official release of fractional scaling on gnome. Ubuntu gnome and kde have official release, not an issue there.

I think we have covered enough feasible support for fractional scaling for now.
author: beelchester

body: @beelchester I found a way provided by `GNOME+Wayland` to get resolution, https://wiki.gnome.org/Initiatives/Wayland/Gaps/DisplayConfig, did you ever try it?
